design_philosophy.core_loop: Select track difficulty, navigate motorcycle through racing stages at escalating speeds, manage turbo mode activation at 280+ km/h to reach higher top speed of 324 km/h, progress through continent-based courses of varying lengths
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure balanced against precision steering; mastery unlocked through reaching turbo speed thresholds; course difficulty scales from Africa (6 stages, easiest) to Europe (18 stages, hardest)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed motorcycle racing with arcade-style sensation; progression from casual rider to expert capable of accessing turbo mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Designed as spiritual successor to original Hang-On with opinionated difficulty scaling via continent selection; Genesis version adds economic layer (sponsor recruitment, component purchases) absent from arcade, creating parallel progression fantasy of bike customization and team management

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with four selectable difficulty tracks (Africa/easiest through Europe/hardest)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players choose track at start (Africa 6 stages vs Europe 18 stages); Genesis version adds economic progression system where earned money purchases bike component upgrades, creating self-directed difficulty scaling

progression.upgrade_system: Genesis exclusive: sponsor recruitment and earned money system enables purchase of enhanced motorcycle components to improve performance
progression.unlock_mechanism: Continent tracks presented as selectable options at start; Genesis version uses alphanumeric password system (two 14-character segments) for save persistence, enabling progression continuity across sessions
replayability_hooks: Four selectable continent tracks with distinct stage counts and difficulty curves
Multiple in-race song selections (borrowed from Out Run)
Genesis exclusive: component upgrade economy incentivizes replay to earn money and customize bike
Turbo mode mastery creates skill-based replayability (reaching 280 km/h unlock condition)

world_building_techniques: Continent-based track selection (Africa, Asia, Americas, Europe) creates implicit geographic progression narrative
Genesis cover art references real-world motorcycle racing context (Shinichi Itoh, Rothmans Honda NSR500 livery)
In-game billboards establish commercial racing sponsorship aesthetic (replaced on Virtual Console with Sega game references to avoid copyright)

faction_indicators: Sponsor recruitment system (Genesis) creates team affiliation mechanics
Motorcycle customization via component purchases suggests rider identity expression
danger_signals: Turbo speed thresholds (280-324 km/h) create mechanical skill gates
Stage attrition increases across continent progression (Africa 6→Europe 18 stages)
environmental_storytelling: Continent selection imparts geographic racing circuit narrative
Rothmans Honda NSR500 livery establishes professional motorsports legitimacy
Commercial billboard presence (real brands in arcade, Sega games in Virtual Console) signals racing circuit authenticity
